summary.intent: Implement a framework-agnostic NavigationMenu core in ars-components, open a PR, and address automated Codex review findings before merging.
summary.outcome: PR #691 was opened, Codex reviewed and raised 7 threads, all were addressed in a follow-up commit fixing controlled value visibility, stale timer guards, registration cleanup, and timestamped Escape helpers. All focused checks (fmt, unit tests, spec-conformance, clippy, coverage, spec validate) passed.

repo: The repo uses a Bindable<T> abstraction (in ars-core) to handle controlled vs uncontrolled state; attrs must source their visible state from the Bindable value, not a separate local field.
Delayed timer and open/close transitions require stale-timer guards to prevent acting on superseded state.
Public keydown helpers (on_trigger_keydown, on_content_keydown) must accept an adapter-provided timestamp for Escape handling rather than deriving their own.
The repo has a snapshot-reviewed label convention that must be applied when a PR adds .snap fixtures.
Codex automated review is triggered via '@codex review' comment; its progress is tracked via GitHub reactions (👀 = reviewing, 👍 = approved) and GraphQL reviewThreads.
The PR validation checklist includes: cargo xci-fast, unit tests, spec_conformance, insta snapshots, spec validate, spec compile-snippets, fmt, clippy, llvm-cov, and mutants.
Mutation tests are explicitly not rerun during a PR review-fix round (per repo convention).
Co-author trailers are expected in fix commits when addressing automated review (Codex gets Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>).

code.finding: Controlled state synchronization and attr visibility logic; Bindable::controlled vs Bindable::uncontrolled initialization pattern lives here.
Delayed timer/open-close transition logic that required stale-timer guards after Codex review.
on_trigger_keydown and on_content_keydown public helpers; Escape handling required timestamp parameter added during review fix.
Bindable<T> struct definition; reference implementation for controlled/uncontrolled state pattern used across all components.
Tabs component as reference for how controlled sync (SyncControlledValue) and open_item patterns are implemented in sibling navigation components.

workflow: Polling Codex review state requires both checking GitHub reactions (for 👀/👍 signals) and the GraphQL reviewThreads endpoint; neither alone is sufficient.
sleep + gh api chained in a single exec_command is the pattern used for timed polling loops without a dedicated monitor.
cargo +nightly fmt --all must be run after apply_patch operations, and --check should be verified before committing.
The receiving-code-review SKILL.md (in the Codex plugins cache) governs the review-fix loop protocol.
Focused test runs (--lib navigation_menu::) are used throughout rather than full workspace runs to keep the iteration loop fast.

friction: Codex review took over 10 minutes (5+ polling intervals of 120s each) before dropping 👀 and posting threads.
An unused-mut warning appeared after behavioral patches and required a separate cleanup apply_patch before clippy passed.
cargo +nightly fmt --all --check failed after the initial patch application, requiring a fmt run and re-check cycle.
Multiple sequential apply_patch calls were needed for the review fixes rather than a single consolidated patch, suggesting the changes were more interconnected than anticipated.
open_items: Mutation tests were explicitly skipped for this review-fix round and should be run before final merge or in a follow-up.
The PR is open but not yet merged; Codex review threads need to be marked resolved after the fix commit is pushed.
CI checks (Unit Tests, Workspace Check) were still running at the time of the last poll; final green CI state was not confirmed in the transcript.
